skill.name: Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDB (.NET)
skill.description: Azure Resource Manager SDK for Cosmos DB in .NET. Use for management plane operations: creating and managing Cosmos DB accounts, databases, containers, throughput settings, and RBAC via Azure Resource Manager.
skill.summary: Management plane SDK for provisioning and managing Azure Cosmos DB resources via Azure Resource Manager with .NET.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of executable code. This is a prompt-only skill providing documentation and examples for Azure Cosmos DB management via .NET SDK. No security risks detected - safe for publication.

content.user_title: Manage Azure Cosmos DB Resources with .NET
content.value_statement: Streamlines Cosmos DB account and resource provisioning through Azure Resource Manager. Eliminates manual Azure Portal configuration with automated .NET SDK workflows.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage Cosmos DB accounts with multiple API types (SQL, MongoDB, Cassandra, Gremlin)
Provision databases, containers, and configure partition keys via Azure Resource Manager
Set up manual or autoscale throughput settings for databases and containers
Manage stored procedures, triggers, and user-defined functions for SQL API
Retrieve account keys and connection strings securely
Configure multi-region replication and failover policies
limitations: Cannot perform data plane operations like document CRUD or queries - use Microsoft.Azure.Cosmos SDK for that
Requires valid Azure subscription and appropriate RBAC permissions
Account creation is a long-running operation that may take several minutes to complete

use_cases.title: Infrastructure as Code for Cosmos DB
use_cases.description: Automate Cosmos DB account and resource provisioning as part of CI/CD pipelines or ARM template deployments.

use_cases.title: Multi-Environment Setup
use_cases.description: Consistently provision identical Cosmos DB configurations across development, staging, and production environments.

use_cases.title: Database Migration Automation
use_cases.description: Script the creation of new Cosmos DB resources with proper throughput, indexing, and partitioning configurations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Cosmos DB Account
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Cosmos DB account named 'my-cosmos-account' in East US region with Session consistency level and automatic failover enabled.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Database and Container
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SQL database named 'mydb' with a container 'mycontainer' using /userId as partition key, 400 RU/s throughput, and 24-hour TTL.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Autoscale Throughput
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the database throughput to use autoscale with a maximum of 4000 RU/s and migrate from manual throughput mode.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Region Replication Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure a Cosmos DB account with write region in East US and read regions in West US and West Europe, enabling automatic failover with priority ordering.

output_examples.input: Create Cosmos DB account with SQL API in East US
output_examples.output: Successfully created Cosmos DB account 'my-cosmos-account' in resource group 'my-rg'. Account kind: GlobalDocumentDB. Consistency: Session. Locations: EastUS (failover priority 0). Status: Succeeded.

output_examples.input: Create container with partition key and autoscale
output_examples.output: Container 'orders' created in database 'salesdb'. Partition key: /customerId. Indexing mode: Consistent. Autoscale max throughput: 5000 RU/s. TTL: 7 days. Status: Succeeded.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication - never hardcode keys or connection strings in your code
Use WaitUntil.Completed for operations that must finish before proceeding, or WaitUntil.Started for parallel operations
Handle RequestFailedException to gracefully manage ARM API errors like resource conflicts or quota limits
anti_patterns: Do not use this SDK for document CRUD operations - use Microsoft.Azure.Cosmos data plane SDK instead
Avoid creating accounts without enabling automatic failover for production workloads requiring high availability
Do not set throughput too low (minimum 400 RU/s) or too high without understanding cost implications

faq.question: What is the difference between management plane and data plane operations?
faq.answer: Management plane (this SDK) handles resource provisioning like creating accounts and databases. Data plane (Microsoft.Azure.Cosmos SDK) handles document operations like reading, writing, and querying data.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure Resource Manager?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which supports multiple authentication methods including managed identity, Azure CLI, Visual Studio credentials, and service principals via environment variables.

faq.question: How long does it take to create a Cosmos DB account?
faq.answer: Account creation is a long-running operation that typically takes 5-15 minutes depending on region availability and account configuration.

faq.question: Can I change the consistency level after creating an account?
faq.answer: Yes, you can update the consistency policy using CreateOrUpdateAsync, but be aware that changing from stronger to weaker consistency may affect existing applications.

faq.question: What happens if I exceed my subscription quota?
faq.answer: You will receive a RequestFailedException with status code 403. Check your subscription limits in the Azure Portal and request quota increases if needed.

faq.question: How do I get the connection string for my application?
faq.answer: Use the GetConnectionStringsAsync method on the CosmosDBAccountResource to retrieve connection strings. Store these securely in Key Vault or application settings.
